agent_benefits: Start tasks with the user's durable preferences, project context, and prior decisions.
Avoid asking the user to repeat context that another connected agent already learned.
Preserve decisions, corrections, artifacts, and daily deltas as RawEvents for Working-Agent governance.
Share useful memory across Codex, Claude, Cursor, Windsurf, and other MCP-capable agents.

context_fields.context_tiers: L0 compressed prompt header, L1 layer summaries, L2 deep refs.
context_fields.context_tree: Directory-style paths for narrowing project/layer/type/memory leaves.
context_fields.memory_evolution: Review, promotion, validity, and compaction hints.
context_fields.memory_layers: working, episodic, semantic, procedural counts and policy.
context_fields.relation_graph: Connected memory nodes and edges for evidence chaining.
context_fields.retrieval_trace: Why each memory was retrieved and scored.

habit_loop.after: Call memory_after_end with concise summary, decisions, actions, and artifacts.
habit_loop.before: Call memory_before_start for nontrivial user/project work.
habit_loop.during: Call memory_search when scope changes or facts are missing.
habit_loop.scheduled: Call memory_upload_daily_delta from daily automation, then verify with memory_sync_status.

memory_access_map.name: Life Memory Access Map

packaged_helpers.automation_templates: references/automation-templates.md provides Codex scheduled task, Windows Task Scheduler, and cron templates.
packaged_helpers.codex_config_writer: scripts/write_codex_mcp_config.py writes Codex config.toml without PowerShell here-string quoting.
packaged_helpers.doctor: scripts/doctor.py checks Python, API reachability, env vars, MCP stdio, memory_map, policy, and sync status.
packaged_helpers.installer: scripts/install_life_memory_skill.py downloads the Skill, creates an agent, writes optional MCP config, and runs smoke tests.
packaged_helpers.payload_validator: scripts/validate_memory_batch.py cleans risky identifiers before memory_sync_existing or memory_upload_daily_delta.

memory_access_map.principle: Agents read committed memory through search/context tools and write only RawEvent-style inputs; the server owns extraction, policy, and durable commits.
quickstart: Run the bootstrap script from the skill package.
Call memory_map immediately after MCP connects.
Use memory_before_start before substantial work and memory_after_end after finishing.
Use memory_sync_existing once for old memories and memory_upload_daily_delta for scheduled updates.

recommended_flow.purpose: Learn tool contracts and context fields.
Search again when scope changes or details are needed.
Preserve raw observations that may become memory.
Write summary, decisions, actions, and artifacts.
Upload new or changed external memories with stable external_id values.
Check processing, duplicate, work-case, and committed counts.

memory_access_map.system_goal: A shared long-term memory layer for agents: read prior user/project context before work, write useful outcomes after work, and sync external agent memories without duplicating or losing provenance.

troubleshooting.codex_config: Use scripts/write_codex_mcp_config.py instead of one-line PowerShell here-strings.
troubleshooting.codex_hot_reload: If config.toml is written but tools are not visible, verify with the bundled stdio smoke test and restart or refresh Codex.
troubleshooting.rtk_powershell: Run PowerShell cmdlets through rtk powershell -NoProfile -Command; do not call cmdlets directly through rtk.
troubleshooting.sync_500: If one item succeeds but a batch fails, use ASCII slug ids, remove raw Windows paths/mojibake from identifier fields, and split batches.
troubleshooting.sync_timeout: For first full memory_sync_existing import, set trigger_extraction=false and verify RawEvents first.
troubleshooting.working_agent_governance: Uploads become RawEvents and only the Working Agent may govern them into formal memories.

write_rules: Use memory_upload_event or memory_after_end for raw session facts.
Use memory_sync_existing for one-time imports and memory_upload_daily_delta for recurring imports.
Use stable external_id for every imported memory so reruns are idempotent.
Use media tools for URLs/files/images/audio/video instead of storing extracted content as facts.

tool_definitions.description: Search Aion Memory Nexus for context relevant to a question or task.
tool_definitions.name: memory_search

tool_definitions.description: Call before starting work to retrieve the most relevant memory context.
tool_definitions.name: memory_before_start

tool_definitions.description: Call after finishing work to save a task summary, decisions, actions, and artifacts.

artifacts.description: Files, URLs, or other artifacts produced.

decisions.description: Important decisions made during the task.

tool_definitions.name: memory_after_end

tool_definitions.description: Append one raw event. The Working Agent will govern it asynchronously through a work case.

dedupe.description: Skip if this agent already uploaded the same content.

source_type.description: agent, mcp, codex, openclaw, chatgpt, or obsidian.

tool_definitions.name: memory_upload_event

tool_definitions.description: Import an external agent's existing memory store as RawEvents for review/extraction.

default_project_id.description: Project applied when an item has no project_id.

memories.description: Memory items to import. Keep batches under 100 items.

source_name.description: Name of the source memory store.

trigger_extraction.description: Start Memory Agent extraction after import. For first full imports, prefer false and verify RawEvents first.

tool_definitions.name: memory_sync_existing

tool_definitions.description: Upload a daily batch of new/changed memories from an external agent.

default_project_id.description: Project applied when an item has no project_id.

memories.description: New or changed memory items since the previous upload.

since.description: Lower-bound timestamp used by the caller for this delta.

source_name.description: Name of the source memory store.

tool_definitions.name: memory_upload_daily_delta

tool_definitions.description: Show recent sync status for the authenticated agent, including RawEvent, work-case, committed, duplicate, and error counts.
tool_definitions.name: memory_sync_status

tool_definitions.description: Show the authenticated agent's server-side write and auto-commit policy summary without exposing tokens.
tool_definitions.name: memory_policy_status

tool_definitions.description: Return the Life Memory access map: recommended tool order, context fields, sync strategy, and write rules for agents.
tool_definitions.name: memory_map

tool_definitions.description: Run a real test chain: upload RawEvent, run Working-Agent governance, report work cases/formal memories, then search for the result.

content.default: Agent Memory Bridge roundtrip test: store a low-risk fact for MCP verification.
content.description: Low-risk test content to write as a RawEvent.

source_name.description: Sync source label for policy matching.

tool_definitions.name: memory_test_roundtrip

tool_definitions.description: List supported agent types and the current authenticated agent type.
tool_definitions.name: memory_list_types

tool_definitions.description: Create a link MediaArtifact for asynchronous webpage extraction. Use this for URLs instead of uploading them as facts.

extract.description: Queue extraction after creating the artifact.

source_text.description: Optional surrounding text from the agent/user.

tool_definitions.name: memory_create_link_artifact

tool_definitions.description: Upload a file, image, table, audio, or video as base64 media for asynchronous extraction.
tool_definitions.name: memory_upload_media_base64

tool_definitions.description: List recent media artifacts and extraction status.
tool_definitions.name: memory_list_media_artifacts

tool_definitions.description: Get safe details for one media artifact, including extracted note summary when available.
tool_definitions.name: memory_get_media_artifact

tool_definitions.description: Synchronously trigger extraction for one media artifact when the caller needs immediate status.
tool_definitions.name: memory_extract_media_artifact
